title: Marketing Analytics for CEP
text:
Below are selected projects from my work with the Trauma Resource Institute (TRI), where I leveraged marketing analytics to evaluate and optimize Google Ad Grants performance. These projects demonstrate my ability to analyze search behavior, assess campaign structure, and translate performance data into actionable recommendations.
By combining statistical analysis, dashboard development, and search query insights, I focused on improving traffic quality, identifying non-brand demand opportunities, and supporting more effective campaign and landing page alignment.

title: Marketing Analytics for CEP
text: What You’ll Find

TRI Google Ad Grants Explorer (Shiny App)

title: TRI Google Ad Grants Dashboard (Static)
text:
Code
library(tidyverse)
library(janitor)
library(scales)
library(stringr)
Code
campaign_files <- c(
  CRM = "/Users/angelaqian/Campaign report (CRM).csv",
  TRM = "/Users/angelaqian/Campaign report (TRM).csv"
)

terms_files <- c(
  CRM = "/Users/angelaqian/Search terms report (CRM).csv",
  TRM = "/Users/angelaqian/Search terms report (TRM).csv"
)

keyword_files <- c(
  CRM = "/Users/angelaqian/Search keyword report (CRM).csv",
  TRM = "/Users/angelaqian/Search keyword report (TRM).csv"
)
Code
read_ads_csv_skip2 <- function(path) {
  if (!file.exists(path)) stop("File not found: ", path)

  txt <- readLines(path, encoding = "UTF-16LE", warn = FALSE)
  txt <- txt[!is.na(txt)]

  tmp <- tempfile(fileext = ".csv")
  writeLines(txt, tmp, useBytes = TRUE)

  df <- readr::read_csv(
    tmp,
    skip = 2,
    show_col_types = FALSE,
    progress = FALSE,
    guess_max = 50000
  ) %>% clean_names()

  if (ncol(df) == 1) {
    stop(
      "Imported as ONE column (bad delimiter/export). Re-export as CSV (comma).\n",
      "Single column name: ", names(df)[1]
    )
  }
  df
}

to_num <- function(x) {
  x <- as.character(x)
  x <- gsub("%", "", x)
  x <- gsub("\\$", "", x)
  x <- gsub(",", "", x)
  suppressWarnings(as.numeric(x))
}

drop_totals <- function(df) {
  nm <- names(df)
  label_col <- intersect(c("campaign","search_term","ad_group","campaign_name","search_terms_report"), nm)
  if (length(label_col) == 0) return(df)
  col <- label_col[1]
  df %>% filter(!str_detect(tolower(.data[[col]]), "^total:"))
}

pick_col <- function(df, candidates) {
  hit <- intersect(candidates, names(df))
  if (length(hit) == 0) return(NA_character_)
  hit[1]
}

standardize_campaign <- function(df) {
  col_campaign <- pick_col(df, c("campaign","campaign_name","campaign_report"))
  col_impr     <- pick_col(df, c("impr","impressions"))
  col_clicks   <- pick_col(df, c("clicks"))
  col_cost     <- pick_col(df, c("cost"))
  col_conv     <- pick_col(df, c("conversions","conv","all_conversions"))
  col_ctr      <- pick_col(df, c("ctr"))
  col_cpc      <- pick_col(df, c("avg_cpc","average_cpc"))
  col_cvr      <- pick_col(df, c("conv_rate","conversion_rate"))
  col_cpa      <- pick_col(df, c("cost_conv","cost_per_conv","cost_per_conversion"))

  if (is.na(col_campaign)) stop("Could not find campaign column. Columns: ", paste(names(df), collapse=", "))

  df %>%
    drop_totals() %>%
    mutate(
      campaign    = as.character(.data[[col_campaign]]),
      clicks      = if (!is.na(col_clicks)) to_num(.data[[col_clicks]]) else NA_real_,
      cost        = if (!is.na(col_cost))   to_num(.data[[col_cost]])   else NA_real_,
      impressions = if (!is.na(col_impr))   to_num(.data[[col_impr]])   else NA_real_,
      conversions = if (!is.na(col_conv))   to_num(.data[[col_conv]])   else 0,
      ctr         = if (!is.na(col_ctr))    to_num(.data[[col_ctr]])    else NA_real_,
      avg_cpc     = if (!is.na(col_cpc))    to_num(.data[[col_cpc]])    else NA_real_,
      conv_rate   = if (!is.na(col_cvr))    to_num(.data[[col_cvr]])    else NA_real_,
      cost_conv   = if (!is.na(col_cpa))    to_num(.data[[col_cpa]])    else NA_real_
    ) %>%
    filter(!is.na(campaign), campaign != "", campaign != "--")
}

standardize_terms <- function(df) {
  col_term     <- pick_col(df, c("search_term","search_terms","term","query","search_terms_report"))
  col_match    <- pick_col(df, c("match_type","keyword_match_type"))
  col_campaign <- pick_col(df, c("campaign","campaign_name"))
  col_impr     <- pick_col(df, c("impr","impressions"))
  col_clicks   <- pick_col(df, c("clicks"))
  col_cost     <- pick_col(df, c("cost"))
  col_conv     <- pick_col(df, c("conversions","conv","all_conversions"))
  col_ctr      <- pick_col(df, c("ctr"))

  if (is.na(col_term)) stop("Could not find search term column. Columns: ", paste(names(df), collapse=", "))

  df %>%
    drop_totals() %>%
    mutate(
      search_term = as.character(.data[[col_term]]),
      match_type  = if (!is.na(col_match)) as.character(.data[[col_match]]) else NA_character_,
      campaign    = if (!is.na(col_campaign)) as.character(.data[[col_campaign]]) else NA_character_,
      clicks      = if (!is.na(col_clicks)) to_num(.data[[col_clicks]]) else NA_real_,
      cost        = if (!is.na(col_cost))   to_num(.data[[col_cost]])   else NA_real_,
      impressions = if (!is.na(col_impr))   to_num(.data[[col_impr]])   else NA_real_,
      conversions = if (!is.na(col_conv))   to_num(.data[[col_conv]])   else 0,
      ctr         = if (!is.na(col_ctr))    to_num(.data[[col_ctr]])    else NA_real_
    ) %>%
    filter(!is.na(search_term), search_term != "", search_term != "--")
}
Code
campaign <- imap_dfr(campaign_files, ~ read_ads_csv_skip2(.x) %>% standardize_campaign() %>% mutate(program = .y))
terms    <- imap_dfr(terms_files,    ~ read_ads_csv_skip2(.x) %>% standardize_terms()    %>% mutate(program = .y))
keywords <- imap_dfr(keyword_files,  ~ read_ads_csv_skip2(.x) %>% clean_names()          %>% mutate(program = .y))

title: TRI Google Ad Grants Dashboard (Static)
text:
Overview

What this dashboard shows
This dashboard summarizes TRI’s Google Ad Grants activity for CRM vs TRM using the latest exports in /data/. It focuses on volume, match-type mix, and top search themes (for non-brand demand + negative keyword opportunities).



Code
kpi <- campaign %>%
  group_by(program) %>%
  summarise(
    clicks = sum(clicks, na.rm=TRUE),
    cost = sum(cost, na.rm=TRUE),
    avg_cpc = cost / clicks,
    conv = sum(conversions, na.rm=TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

knitr::kable(
  kpi %>% mutate(cost = dollar(cost), avg_cpc = dollar(avg_cpc), clicks = comma(clicks), conv = comma(conv)),
  align = "l"
)

clicks
cost
avg_cpc

title: TRI Google Ad Grants Dashboard (Static)
text:
Campaign Performance (CRM vs TRM)

Clicks by campaign


Code
p_clicks <- campaign %>%
  group_by(program, campaign) %>%
  summarise(clicks = sum(clicks, na.rm=TRUE), .groups="drop") %>%
  arrange(program, clicks) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = clicks, y = fct_reorder(campaign, clicks))) +
  geom_col() +
  facet_wrap(~program, scales="free_y") +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = comma) +
  labs(title = "Clicks by Campaign", x = "Clicks", y = NULL)
p_clicks











Spend by campaign


Code
p_cost <- campaign %>%
  group_by(program, campaign) %>%
  summarise(cost = sum(cost, na.rm=TRUE), .groups="drop") %>%
  arrange(program, cost) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = cost, y = fct_reorder(campaign, cost))) +
  geom_col() +
  facet_wrap(~program, scales="free_y") +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = dollar) +
  labs(title = "Spend by Campaign", x = "Cost ($)", y = NULL)
p_cost

title: TRI Google Ad Grants Dashboard (Static)
text:
Match-type mix (quality proxy)

Click share by match type (CRM vs TRM)


Code
match_share_prog <- terms %>%
  filter(!is.na(match_type), match_type != "") %>%
  group_by(program, match_type) %>%
  summarise(clicks = sum(clicks, na.rm=TRUE), .groups="drop") %>%
  group_by(program) %>%
  mutate(share = clicks / sum(clicks)) %>%
  ungroup()

p_match_prog <- ggplot(match_share_prog, aes(x = share, y = fct_reorder(match_type, share))) +
  geom_col() +
  facet_wrap(~program) +
  geom_text(aes(label = percent(share, accuracy = 0.1)), hjust = -0.1, size=3.5) +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = percent, expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.2))) +
  labs(title = "Match-Type Mix by Program", subtitle = "Broad vs Phrase vs Exact", x = "Share of Clicks", y = NULL)

p_match_prog

title: TRI Google Ad Grants Dashboard (Static)
text:
Search Demand Themes (top queries)

Top search terms by program


Code
top_terms_prog <- terms %>%
  group_by(program, search_term) %>%
  summarise(clicks = sum(clicks, na.rm=TRUE), .groups="drop") %>%
  arrange(program, desc(clicks)) %>%
  group_by(program) %>%
  slice_head(n = 8) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(search_term = str_wrap(search_term, 28))

p_terms_prog <- ggplot(top_terms_prog, aes(x = clicks, y = fct_reorder(search_term, clicks))) +
  geom_col() +
  facet_wrap(~program, scales="free_y") +
  geom_text(aes(label = comma(clicks)), hjust = -0.1, size=3.3) +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = comma, expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.2))) +
  labs(title = "Top Search Terms by Program", subtitle = "Good for non-brand themes + negatives", x = "Clicks", y = NULL)

p_terms_prog















Note



Interpretation notes

Broad match can uncover new demand, but usually needs strong negative keywords to avoid low-intent clicks.
If conversions show as 0 in export, treat this as a traffic-mix scan (not ROI). :::

title: Technical Projects & Presentations
text: Below are selected projects that showcase my work in strategic marketing, campaign development, analytics, and data-informed decision making.

title: Technical Projects & Presentations
section: TRI Google Ads Optimization & Campaign Strategy Grad Capstone Project (Fall 2025 - Present)
text:
TRI Google Ads Optimization & Campaign Strategy Grad Capstone Project (Fall 2025 - Present)
For my client project with the Trauma Resource Institute (TRI), I am leading the development and optimization of Google Ads campaigns to improve lead generation and maximize the impact of the Google Ads grant.
This work involves auditing historical campaign performance, restructuring campaigns based on user intent, and aligning keywords, ad copy, and landing pages to drive higher-quality conversions.

 

Group 7 Final Capstone Presentation

title: Technical Projects & Presentations
section: Data Analysis Project: Exploratory Data & Insights (Fall 2024)
text:
Data Analysis Project: Exploratory Data & Insights (Fall 2024)
I led the sourcing and development of a dataset-driven analysis project using R and Quarto. I independently identified and curated a real-world animal shelter dataset, then collaborated with my team to clean, analyze, and interpret the data to uncover meaningful insights.
This project focused on understanding the factors that influence animal adoption outcomes. Through data preparation, feature engineering, and statistical analysis, I examined how variables such as breed type, intake condition, age, and intake source impact the likelihood of adoption.
The analysis included exploratory data analysis, correlation analysis, and logistic regression modeling. During modeling, I identified multicollinearity issues across predictors, which informed how results were interpreted.

title: Technical Projects & Presentations
section: Beyond Meat: Digital Marketing & Competitive Strategy Analysis (Fall 2025)
text:
Beyond Meat: Digital Marketing & Competitive Strategy Analysis (Fall 2025)
In this project, I analyzed how Beyond Meat can shift consumer behavior and increase adoption of plant-based alternatives through targeted digital marketing and strategic positioning.
I focused specifically on evaluating digital marketing practices and the competitive landscape, identifying how Beyond Meat can compete against both traditional meat products and other plant-based brands through pricing, messaging, and channel strategy.
Building on consumer behavior frameworks, I explored how marketing tactics such as search ads, retail media, and social content can influence perception, reduce adoption barriers, and drive repeat purchase behavior.

 


 View Full Report

title: Technical Projects & Presentations
section: Demand Forecasting with Regression & Machine Learning in the Construction Industry (Spring 2025)
text:
Demand Forecasting with Regression & Machine Learning in the Construction Industry (Spring 2025)
This project explores demand forecasting in a construction machinery context using both traditional statistical models and advanced machine learning techniques.
Based on a real-world study, I analyzed how different modeling approaches-including linear regression, nonlinear regression, artificial neural networks (ANN), and support vector regression (SVR)-perform in predicting spare parts demand.
The analysis integrates multiple data sources, including historical ERP sales data, global machinery sales trends, exchange rates, and engineered variables such as a monthly impact rate to capture seasonality and demand fluctuations.


 

AI Construction by Angela Qian

title: Technical Projects & Presentations
text:
Dave’s Hot Chicken Marketing Campaign (Fall 2024)
This project presents an integrated marketing campaign strategy for Dave’s Hot Chicken, focused on expanding brand reach while maintaining its authentic, high-engagement social media presence.
The campaign combines organic social media growth, paid advertising, and brand experience strategies to strengthen customer engagement and drive long-term brand loyalty.

Professional Summary
Digital Marketing Analytics graduate student with a background in employee benefits, client relations, reporting, and financial analysis. Experienced in supporting large group accounts, preparing client-facing deliverables, and translating complex information into clear, actionable insights. Interested in applying data, analytics, and technology to improve strategy and reporting within the insurance and employee benefits space.

title: Professional Resume
text:
Experience

EPIC Insurance Brokers & Consultants
Client Coordinator, Employee Benefits
January 2024 – August 2024

Served as the primary point of contact for Client Executives, directly reporting on all requested deliverables to streamline compliance processes and optimize the delivery of marketing materials to clients, ensuring consistent alignment with regulatory standards and client expectations.
Strategically managed a diverse portfolio of approximately 15 large group accounts, each with over 500 employees, alongside multiple smaller divisions, providing personalized support and proactive account management to maintain client satisfaction and retention.
Provided expert assistance in resolving complex client and employee issues, including benefit claim disputes, eligibility concerns, and policy inquiries, ensuring timely resolution and maintaining strong client relationships.
Cultivated and sustained successful, mutually beneficial relationships with a wide network of stakeholders, including clients, insurance carriers, and third-party administrators, fostering collaboration and clear communication.
Leveraged industry-leading BenAdmin platforms, including BenefitPoint, to accurately manage and maintain all policy details, commission structures, and documentation, ensuring transparency, compliance, and data integrity.
Designed and delivered comprehensive financial reports, renewal presentations, and client brochures, incorporating key marketing metrics, benchmarking data, and commission analysis to support informed decision-making and client retention strategies.



Suvaun, Acrisure LLC Agency Partner
Marketing Account Coordinator
March 2020 – January 2024

Spearheaded and led comprehensive pre-sale marketing initiatives for small accounts, driving targeted outreach strategies that effectively captured and engaged prospective clients while maximizing lead conversion.
Strategically managed the end-to-end Request for Proposal (RFP) marketing process, guiding broker users from initial engagement through successful implementation, ensuring a seamless and efficient experience that met client expectations.
Cultivated and maintained strong, strategic relationships with a diverse range of carrier vendors, serving as a trusted liaison between carriers and clients, facilitating clear communication, negotiation, and conflict resolution.
Collaborated cross-functionally with an international outsourcing team, providing expert guidance on leveraging advanced features within Suvaun, overseeing task execution to ensure accuracy, efficiency, and adherence to best practices.
Delivered comprehensive training and onboarding programs for both internal team members and external broker partners, enhancing user proficiency and fostering a strong foundation for long-term success.



L.A. Pinnacle Insurance Agency
Benefits Marketing & Admin Assistant
April 2019 – March 2020

Supported administration of group and individual health insurance plans, ensuring accurate enrollments, terminations, and policy updates.
Managed day-to-day client and employee requests, including claims support, eligibility verification, and billing inquiries.
Coordinated with carriers and vendors to resolve coverage issues and maintain compliance with policy requirements.
Prepared renewal comparisons, rate summaries, and marketing packages to support broker recommendations.
Utilized Excel and Word & Brown to organize client data, track plan details, and streamline reporting processes.

title: Welcome to my Portfolio
text: I’m a Digital Marketing Analytics graduate student pursuing opportunities at the intersection of data, technology, and insurance. With a foundation in client relations, I specialize in transforming complex data into clear, actionable insights that support strategic decision-making and improve customer outcomes.

title: Welcome to my Portfolio
text:
About Me
I’m currently pursuing a Master of Science in Digital Marketing (Marketing Analytics) at Cal Poly Pomona, and hold a Bachelor of Arts in Communications from UC San Diego. I have a background in client relations within the insurance industry, where I supported large group accounts and developed experience in data analysis, reporting, and strategic decision-making.
Outside of work, I enjoy exploring new food spots, attending concerts and music festivals, and spending time with my two cats. I love experiences that bridge creativity, community, and culture.

title: Welcome to my Portfolio
text:
What I Do
I focus on answering questions like:

How can data improve client strategy and decision-making in employee benefits?
How can we better analyze plan performance, costs, and trends across large group accounts?
How can reporting be streamlined to deliver clearer insights for clients and stakeholders?
How can technology and analytics tools be used to modernize traditionally manual processes?
How can we turn complex benefits data into clear, actionable insights?
